Toast.error-occurred: An error occurred. Please try again later.

Tips.lookupable-text: Click on any Hán-Nôm character to look it up in the dictionaries.

ErrorPage.not-found-message: The page you are looking for does not exist.

Home.welcome-subtitle: A digital hub to study pre-modern and modern Vietnam
Home.about-digitizing-vietnam-content: Digitizing Vietnam marks a digital leap forward in Vietnam Studies with the Columbia-Fulbright collaboration. The joint venture started with the memorandum of understanding between two universities in 2022. Weatherhead East Asian Institute of Columbia and Vietnam Studies Center of Fulbright will be spearheading research in the field of Vietnam studies, and Digitizing Vietnam will be one among many collaborative endeavors to come in the future.
Home.our-collections-content: Do you want to try reading Hán-Nôm scripts, or to see how chữ quốc ngữ was used in the early 20th century? Our eclectic collections range from pre-modern Hán-Nôm texts to the first Vietnamese women's magazine. While by no means comprehensive, they provide rarely seen snapshots of Vietnamese history.
Home.our-blog-content: The blog is a special part in this digital hub for studies on both premodern and modern Vietnam. Here, you will find scholarly research as well as non-specialist insights related to Vietnam's historical and cultural evolution. We also invite you to contribute to the discussion on our collections, and share any insights you might have.

Collection.title: Our Collections
Collection.subtitle: Explore a range of free-access digitized collections focused on various aspects of premodern, modern, and contemporary Vietnam.

Outreach.title: Pedagogical Library
Outreach.subtitle: Our outreach initiatives aim to bridge academic research with broader communities through diverse resources, including podcasts, video lessons, curated primary materials, and featured interviews with a diversity of scholars on Vietnam.

Pedagogy.subtitle: Discover and teach Vietnam Studies with impact. Explore curated syllabi, lesson plans, and multimedia resources designed to support innovative and inclusive learning experiences.

teaching-vietnamese-language.title: Teaching Vietnamese Language
teaching-vietnamese-language.description: A curated hub for lesson planning, curriculum design, classroom resources, and professional learning to support Vietnamese language instruction.

teaching-vietnamese-studies.title: Teaching Vietnamese Studies
teaching-vietnamese-studies.description: A curated hub of syllabi and multimedia resources to teach Vietnamese Studies with impact.

Highlight.title: What's new
Highlight.subtitle: Latest news and discoveries from the digital front of Vietnamese heritage.

OnlineResource.title: Online Resources
OnlineResource.subtitle: This curated collection offers a solid starting point for research on Vietnam, with materials designed to support diverse academic interests.
OnlineResource.no-resource-message: There are no resources available for this category.

Tools.subtitle: Explore, create, or contribute to an innovative research hub, each organized around a scholarly topic or theme. Hubs include curated collections, resources, and cutting-edge digital tools designed by our scholarly community to conduct research using digital, computational, and experimental AI methods.

image-ocr-platform.name: Image OCR Platform
image-ocr-platform.description: The Image OCR Platform is a tool that allows users to upload images of text and convert them into machine-readable text. This tool is especially useful for researchers who need to transcribe text from images of historical documents.

han-nom-dictionaries.name: Hán-Nôm Dictionaries
han-nom-dictionaries.description: The Hán-Nôm Dictionaries tool is a collection of dictionaries that provide definitions and explanations for Hán-Nôm characters. This tool is especially useful for researchers who need to look up the meanings of Hán-Nôm characters in historical texts.

writing-pad.title: Draw a Han Nom character
writing-pad.description: Use your finger or mouse to draw a Han Nom character in the box below. When you're done, click Recognize to see matching results. Select the correct character to input it into the search box. Click Clear to erase the writing canvas.
writing-pad.note: Use your finger or mouse to draw a Han Nom character in the box below. When you're done, click Recognize to see matching results. Select the correct character to input it into the search box. Click Clear to erase the writing canvas. Currently, this feature only supports writing Han characters and phonetic-loan Nôm characters.

alternative-input-methods.title: Other input methods
alternative-input-methods.description: Select an input method to add Hán-Nôm characters from Quốc Ngữ, handwriting, or radicals.
alternative-input-methods.quoc-ngu-description: Type a Vietnamese word in Quốc Ngữ to find matching Hán-Nôm characters.
alternative-input-methods.handwriting-description: Draw a character on the canvas and use handwriting recognition to find it. Currently supports Hán characters and phonetic-loan Nôm characters.
alternative-input-methods.radical-description: Browse characters by selecting a radical component, then narrow by stroke count.

general.name: Unified Hán-Nôm Lookup
general.description: Search across multiple Hán-Nôm dictionaries at once by Hán-Nôm character, Quốc Ngữ reading, or component combination.
general.search-placeholder: Hán Nôm, Quốc Ngữ, or Components

tu-dien-chu-nom-dan-giai.name: Tự Điển Chữ Nôm Dẫn Giải (The Dictionary of Nôm Characters with Quotations and Annotations)
tu-dien-chu-nom-dan-giai.description: To date, The Dictionary of Nôm Characters with Quotations and Annotations by Prof. Nguyễn Quang Hồng is the largest dictionary on Chữ Nôm, containing nearly 10,000 entries extracted by the author from 124 ancient works and texts from various periods, fields, and genres. The dictionary is systematically and scientifically structured into two columns: one column identifies the character form, pronunciation, and code of the character, while the other analyzes the structure of the Chữ Nôm character, provides its meaning, and includes relevant contextual examples. Special attention is given to the variations of Chữ Nôm characters, with each entry possibly having one or more variant forms that are clearly recorded from specific textual sources. This is a significant highlight of this dictionary.
tu-dien-chu-nom-dan-giai.search-placeholder: Search Hán Nôm or Quốc Ngữ
tu-dien-chu-nom-dan-giai.author: Prof. Nguyễn Quang Hồng

giup-doc-nom-va-han-viet.name: Giúp Đọc Nôm và Hán Việt (Nôm and Sino-Vietnamese Pronunciation Guide)
giup-doc-nom-va-han-viet.description: The Nôm and Sino-Vietnamese Pronunciation Guide by Father Anthony Trần Văn Kiệm consists of two main parts: Part I introduces and lists the radicals used in writing Hán-Nôm characters, followed by an arrangement of Hán-Nôm characters classified by radicals and stroke count. This method of radicals organizing Hán-Nôm characters allows readers to quickly identify the pronunciation of a character in both Sino-Vietnamese and modern Vietnamese, thereby enabling them to easily find the corresponding meaning in Part II. Part II provides the pronunciation and meaning of each Hán-Nôm character. The pronunciation section includes the Beijing Mandarin pronunciation (transcribed in Pinyin, which the author refers to as Phanh Âm), the Sino-Vietnamese pronunciation, and the Vietnamese (Nôm) reading. This is undoubtedly one of the rare and valuable books for referencing and self-studying Hán-Nôm that anyone passionate about learning these ancient scripts should read.
giup-doc-nom-va-han-viet.search-placeholder: Search Hán Nôm or Quốc Ngữ
giup-doc-nom-va-han-viet.author: Father Anthony Trần Văn Kiệm

nguyen-trai-quoc-am-tu-dien.name: Nguyễn Trãi Quốc Âm Từ Điển
nguyen-trai-quoc-am-tu-dien.description: Nguyễn Trãi Quốc Âm Dictionary is a work by Dr. Trần Trọng Dương, dedicated to the study of the language used in Quốc Âm Thi Tập — the only surviving collection of Nôm poems by Nguyễn Trãi, composed in the 15th century. The dictionary not only examines ancient Vietnamese vocabulary but also preserves many aspects of medieval Vietnamese culture and customs. The work focuses on explaining and tracing the origins of over 2,500 entries, including words, idioms, and allusions, across 12,000 syllable occurrences in Nguyễn Trãi’s poetry, helping readers gain deeper insights into the language and culture of his time. With both scholarly and practical value, this dictionary is a valuable resource for the study, teaching, and learning of Vietnam's classical literature.
nguyen-trai-quoc-am-tu-dien.search-placeholder: Search Hán Nôm or Quốc Ngữ
nguyen-trai-quoc-am-tu-dien.author: Trần Trọng Dương

nhat-dung-thuong-dam.name: Nhật Dụng Thường Đàm Dictionary
nhat-dung-thuong-dam.description: Nhật Dụng Thường Đàm is a small Sino-Vietnamese bilingual dictionary compiled by Phạm Đình Hổ (1768–1839), consisting of 32 thematic categories such as Astronomy, Morality, Confucianism, Food, and Beasts, with Han characters explained through Nom script. Though limited in scope, it effectively introduces common words and phrases and serves as a valuable resource for comparing Hán and Nôm scripts. The dictionary, engraved in the 4th year of Tự Đức by Đồng Văn Trai printing house, is currently preserved at the National Library of Vietnam (catalog number R.1726) and contains 52 sheets printed on dó paper. Each entry includes a Hán character and its corresponding explanation in Nôm script, illustrating the structure and function of bilingual entries.
nhat-dung-thuong-dam.search-placeholder: Search Hán Nôm, Quốc Ngữ or Hán Việt
nhat-dung-thuong-dam.author: Lê Văn Cường (phiên âm, khảo dị), Giáp Thị Hải Chi (English)

taberd.name: Taberd Dictionary
taberd.description: The Dictionarium Anamitico Latinum, first published in 1884 and reprinted in 2004, was compiled by French bishop A.J.L. Taberd and records both Nom and early Quốc Ngữ scripts. It preserves many archaic Vietnamese words, serving as an essential resource for studying and safeguarding the history of Vietnamese writing. The online version, digitized from the 2004 edition, allows searches by radical-stroke or Quốc Ngữ glyphs and includes references to plants, herbs, and other historical details. Though some errors may occur, it remains a practical tool for scholars and language enthusiasts, with reader feedback encouraged for improvement.
taberd.search-placeholder: Search Hán Nôm or Quốc Ngữ
taberd.author: Aditum A J. L. Taberd

tu-dien-chu-nom-tay.name: A Dictionary of Nôm Tày
tu-dien-chu-nom-tay.description: The Tày people have their own form of Nôm script, developed from adaptations of Chinese characters to record the Tày language, and it has been used in literary creation for centuries. Tày Nôm once played an important role in cultural and linguistic life, and many valuable manuscripts are still preserved today in the northern mountainous provinces. In the early 2000s, a group of authors in Cao Bằng compiled the Dictionary of Nôm Tày, which was reviewed and supported for publication by the Institute of Hán-Nôm Studies, providing a useful research tool for the academic community. The book reflects the authors’ dedication to preserving and promoting the cultural heritage of the Tày people. The digitized version of this dictionary was produced as part of the Vietnam Nôm Preservation Foundation's (USA) effort to preserve the Nôm script heritage.
tu-dien-chu-nom-tay.search-placeholder: Search Hán Nôm or Quốc Ngữ
tu-dien-chu-nom-tay.author: Hoàng Triều Ân (ed.), Dương Nhật Thanh - Hoàng Tuấn Nam

kieu-tools.name: Kiều Tools
kieu-tools.description: The Kiều Tools is a collection of tools that allow users to analyze and explore the text of Nguyễn Du's Truyện Kiều. This tool is especially useful for researchers who need to analyze the text of Truyện Kiều in detail.

glossary.name: Glossary of Nôm characters in a version of Kiều
glossary.description: This page lists occurrences of all Nôm characters and their frequencies in a selected version of Kiều.

glossary-all.name: Glossary of Nôm characters in all versions of Kiều
glossary-all.description: This page lists occurrences of all Nôm characters and their frequencies in all versions of Kiều.

han-nom-tools.name: Hán-Nôm Tools
han-nom-tools.description: The Hán-Nôm Toolkit is a suite of digital tools designed to support the analysis of classical Chinese and Nôm texts in Vietnam’s literary heritage. Tailored for advanced research, it enables text processing, search, and structural analysis, making it an essential resource for scholars in linguistics, literature, and cultural history. The platform plays a key role in preserving and promoting Hán-Nôm heritage in the digital era.

han-nom-input-method-editor.name: Hán-Nôm Input Tool
han-nom-input-method-editor.description: Unlike traditional Han-Nom input methods, this tool allows users to type Hán and Nôm characters directly in a web browser without the need to install any software. Thanks to its high flexibility, the tool can be used on any internet-connected device, offering maximum convenience for users in searching and composing Hán-Nôm texts. It serves as a practical solution for both learners and researchers who require quick and efficient interaction with classical texts.

han-nom-from-quoc-ngu.name: Hán-Nôm from Quốc Ngữ
han-nom-from-quoc-ngu.description: Convert Quốc Ngữ text to Hán-Nôm characters using an intelligent sentence-based input method. Type full sentences in Vietnamese romanization and get instant character suggestions with compound word support.

nom-character-classification-and-analysi.name: Nôm Character Classification and Analysis Tool
nom-character-classification-and-analysi.description: This is a cutting-edge tool designed to assist researchers working with Hán-Nôm texts in compiling statistics and classifying Nôm characters based on the criteria outlined in Tự điển Chữ Nôm Dẫn giải (Explained Dictionary of Nom Characters) by scholar Nguyễn Quang Hồng. Users simply upload a Hán-Nôm text, and the tool automatically processes it to generate a detailed statistical report along with classifications of the Nôm characters found in the document. It serves as a valuable resource for linguistic, scriptural, and textual studies related to the Hán-Nôm heritage.

radicals.name: Nom Radical Lookup
radicals.no-characters-found: No characters found for this radical.
radicals.select-stroke-to-view: Select a stroke count to view characters

quoc-ngu-input.placeholder: Type a word in Quốc Ngữ...

kim-han-nom.name: Kim Hán Nôm
kim-han-nom.description: Kim Hán Nôm is a tool designed to support the translation of Hán-Nôm (classical Chinese and demotic Vietnamese) texts, developed by faculty members from the Faculty of Information Technology and the Center for Computational Linguistics at the University of Science – Vietnam National University, Ho Chi Minh City (VNU-HCM). By applying artificial intelligence, Kim Hán Nôm offers two main functions: the recognition and translation of Hán-Nôm texts. Specifically, the tool can identify and translate standard literary texts (such as books and stories), administrative documents (such as imperial edicts and royal decrees), and inscriptions in public spaces (such as temples, pagodas, and shrines).

digital-humanities-tools.name: Digital Sandbox
digital-humanities-tools.description: Digital Sandbox is a space for sharing digital humanities tools contributed by the community. Here, researchers, educators, and students can showcase the tools they have developed to reach a broader community in the field of Vietnamese studies.
digital-humanities-tools.no-tools-message: No digital humanities tools available at the moment.

date-converter.name: Date Converter
date-converter.description: Convert between reign names (niên hiệu), sexagenary years (can chi), and Common Era dates across Vietnamese and Chinese dynasties.

hero.title: Explore the Treasure of Vietnamese Folk Wisdom
hero.subtitle: From Folk Verses, Idioms, Proverbs, to Fairy Tales
hero.intro: Please select one of the features below to start your experience.

sidebar.tools-item1: Viet Chuong Idioms, Proverbs & Folk Verses Dictionary

dictionary.title: Dictionary of Vietnamese Idioms, Proverbs, and Folk Verses
dictionary.bullet1: Introduction to idioms, proverbs, and folk verses

fairy-tales.title: Compilation of Vietnamese Fairy Tales
fairy-tales.description: Use semantic search technology to search fairy tales visually and deeply.

qa.title: Folk Verse Q&A
qa.description: Look up idioms, proverbs, and folk verses from the book series by author Viet Chuong with scanned images of the original pages

learning.title: Learn Idioms, Proverbs, and Folk Verses
learning.description: Use semantic search technology to search folk verses visually and deeply.

tu-dien.title: Dictionary of Vietnamese Idioms, Proverbs & Folk Poetry
tu-dien.intro: All entries in this section are retrieved from the comprehensive book "Từ Điển Thành Ngữ Tục Ngữ Ca Dao Việt Nam" compiled by author Việt Chương (including both Upper and Lower volumes). The system dynamically matches entries with their original scanned book.
tu-dien.explorer_subtitle: Explore the subtle differences between Proverbs and Idioms, the magic of transforming Folk Poetry into Folk Songs, and the traditional characteristics of Folk Literature.
tu-dien.proverbs_intro_desc: To this day, many collections mix proverbs with idioms. However, based on their content and structure, we can clearly distinguish these two concepts.
tu-dien.proverb_def_body: A proverb is a sentence that itself fully expresses an idea, an observation, an experience, a moral principle, a common truth, or a judgment. Proverbs have a complete meaning, summarizing daily life and production experiences to guide people.
tu-dien.idiom_def_body: An idiom is a ready-made phrase, a familiar part of a sentence that many people are accustomed to using, but on its own, it cannot express a complete idea. Idioms focus on describing states or images with color.
tu-dien.interactive_flip_title: Hover or tap on the cards to see the analysis
tu-dien.example_proverb_1_desc: Fully reflects the reality that dogs tend to bark/bite when they see someone dressed in rags. Metaphorically, it describes how the poor often encounter compounding misfortunes and bad luck.
tu-dien.example_proverb_2_desc: Fully expresses a solution-based meaning: a bizarre or severe illness requires an equally extraordinary, corresponding remedy for treatment.
tu-dien.example_idiom_1_desc: Merely represents two images depicting poverty in general, but how poor they are or why is not fully concluded as a complete thought.
tu-dien.example_idiom_2_desc: A descriptive phrase depicting extreme, absolute ignorance. On its own, it does not constitute a complete sentence.
tu-dien.example_idiom_3_desc: Describes a comfortable, leisurely, and comfortable lifestyle, but it is only a phrase and lacks a full conclusion or judgment.
tu-dien.example_idiom_4_desc: Describes the status and snobbish habit of someone who likes to dominate others and act condescendingly, but on its own, it does not convey a complete thought.
tu-dien.poetry_intro_title: The Transition from Folk Poetry to Folk Songs
tu-dien.poetry_intro_desc: The boundary between folk poetry (ca dao) and folk songs (dân ca) is very thin. Folk poetry refers to folk verses (whether forming melodies or not), while Folk Songs are songs with a definite musical tune, leaning heavily towards musicality in form. Folk songs are sung in specific contexts, occupations, or regions.
tu-dien.poetry_transition_title: The Art of Vocal Padding & Repetitions
tu-dien.poetry_transition_desc: When converting folk poetry into folk songs, people creatively add padding words (vocables, auxiliary words), vocal glides, repetitions, and refrains to match the musical rhythm. Below are examples showing this transition:
tu-dien.prop_collective_desc: The primary characteristic distinguishing folk literature from authored works. Works are created collectively by the people, and continually refined and enriched across regions and generations.
tu-dien.prop_oral_desc: Historically, oral transmission was the sole channel. Today, despite rising literacy and written records, oral transmission continues naturally, showing its enduring vitality.
tu-dien.quote_bogatyrev_title: Observation by an unknown Soviet literary scholar:
tu-dien.quote_bogatyrev_body: “Works of folk literature often exist for a very long time, passed down from one mouth to another, and are continuously built upon and enriched by many generations... While written literary works basically remain unchanged once they leave the author's pen, oral folk poetry is passed from generation to generation and is constantly revised to reflect the changes occurring in the thoughts of the masses and according to their aesthetic rules...”
tu-dien.quote_chicherov_title: Observation by Vladimir Ivanovich CHICHEROV (Soviet literary theorist):
tu-dien.quote_chicherov_body: “In our era, folk poetry exists both orally and in written form. This is entirely natural and consistent with development laws; it reflects the perfecting process of the people's artistic literature.”
tu-dien.coming_soon_desc: The Dictionary & Search feature (connected to Viet Chuong's books) is currently under development and will be released soon.

hoc-ca-dao.title: Learn Idioms, Proverbs, and Folk Poetry
hoc-ca-dao.intro: All data in this section is extracted from the book "Thi Ca Bình Dân Việt Nam" collected and compiled by Nguyễn Tấn Long and Phan Canh, published in 1975, digitized by the Tve-4u community.
hoc-ca-dao.error_message: Some words are incorrect. Please tap on them to select again!
hoc-ca-dao.success_message: Congratulations! You have correctly completed this folk verse.

han-nom.title: Hán-Nôm Studies
han-nom.description: Our pilot initiative, the Hán-Nôm Research Hub, features a fully integrated mirror of Columbia University’s Hán-Nôm Sino-Vietnamese Textual Library, as well as a suite of digital tools for textual analysis, including automated OCR, a searchable dictionary platform, manuscript notation, and an interface with the MARKUS and IMMARKUS tools for sinological research.

ca-dao.title: Ca Dao Tục Ngữ
ca-dao.description: A digital space for exploring and studying Vietnamese folk verses (ca dao), proverbs, idioms, and folktales, utilizing semantic search and digital dictionary tools.

vietnam-war.title: Vietnam War Studies
vietnam-war.description: Archival materials and digital resources on the Second Indochina War.

linguistics.title: Linguistics
linguistics.description: Computational linguistics, phonology, and language preservation tools.

tools-section.title: Digital Tools
tools-section.description: The heart of Digitizing Vietnam lies in the development of new digital and AI tools for cutting-edge research on the Humanities and Social Sciences. We welcome collaboration with other centers of digital or AI innovation, and invite scholars with projects of their own to partner with us in developing their own tools through Digitizing Vietnam. All tools listed here may also appear in thematically relevant research hubs.

intro.title: The Digital Gateway to Hán-Nôm Studies
intro.description: Our pilot initiative, the Hán-Nôm Research Hub, features a fully integrated mirror of Columbia University’s Hán-Nôm Sino-Vietnamese Textual Library, as well as a suite of digital tools for textual analysis, including automated OCR, a searchable dictionary platform, manuscript notation, and an interface with the MARKUS and IMMARKUS tools for sinological research.

DateConverter.title: Date Converter
DateConverter.subtitle: Convert between reign names (niên hiệu), sexagenary years (can chi), and CE dates
DateConverter.toggle-chinese: Include Chinese dynasties (Tang, Song, Yuan, Ming, Qing)

gregorian-tab.description: Enter a Gregorian date (day and month optional) to find the corresponding Vietnamese reign period(s), lunisolar date, and sexagenary year. Multiple results indicate concurrent dynasties.
gregorian-tab.no-reign-found: No reign period found for this year.

reign-tab.description: Search by reign name (Vietnamese, Hán characters, or ruler name), or browse by dynasty. Add a lunar month and day to get the exact Gregorian date.
reign-tab.search-placeholder: e.g. Gia Long, 嘉隆, Hồng Đức…
reign-tab.lunar-section: Lunar date (optional — for exact Gregorian date)
reign-tab.invalid-lunar: Invalid lunar date — this intercalary month may not exist in the given year.

sexagenary-tab.description: Select a sexagenary year name (can chi) to find all matching CE years. Only valid stem–branch pairings are possible (same even/odd index).
sexagenary-tab.invalid-combo: Invalid stem–branch combination. Stems and branches must share the same even/odd index.

notes.title: Notes on Vietnamese dating conventions
notes.reign-numbering: Year 1 of a reign corresponds to the CE year in which the reign began. Year N = start year + (N − 1).
notes.lunisolar: Vietnamese documents use the lunisolar calendar. Lunar month/day conversions use the Hồ Ngọc Đức algorithm (UTC+7). Accuracy is ±1 day for dates before c. 1500 CE.
notes.new-year: The Vietnamese New Year (Tết) falls in late January or early February. Dates in January–February of a CE year may belong to the previous Vietnamese lunar year.
notes.overlapping: During 1533–1592 the Mạc dynasty and the Later Lê restoration each issued concurrent reign names. A document's political allegiance determines which system it follows.
notes.intercalary: A leap month (tháng nhuận) is added roughly every 2–3 years. Not every month number has an intercalary counterpart in a given year.
notes.sexagenary: The 60-year cycle (lục thập hoa giáp) anchors to 4 CE (Giáp Tý). Only same-parity stem–branch pairings are valid, yielding 60 unique combinations.

Periodicals.title: Periodicals
Periodicals.description: A curated collection of academic journals and magazines essential for Hán-Nôm and pre-modern Vietnamese studies.

tap-chi-han-nom.name: Tạp chí Hán Nôm
tap-chi-han-nom.description: The primary scholarly journal of the Institute of Sino-Nôm Studies in Hanoi, featuring research on manuscripts, linguistics, and historical texts.

befeo.name: BEFEO
befeo.description: Bulletin de l'École française d'Extrême-Orient, a world-renowned journal for the study of Asian civilizations, with extensive archives on Vietnamese history.

jvs.name: Journal of Vietnamese Studies
jvs.description: A peer-reviewed academic journal published by the University of California Press, covering all aspects of Vietnamese history, culture, and society.

nom-na-tong.name: Nôm Na Tống
nom-na-tong.description: The standard font from the Nôm Preservation Foundation, designed for readability and accuracy in historical texts.

instructions.title: How to Download & Install
instructions.windows: 1. Download: On the GitHub page, find the 'Assets' section and click on the .ttf file. 2. Install: Right-click the downloaded file and select 'Install'.
instructions.mac: 1. Download: On the GitHub page, find the 'Assets' section and click the .ttf file. 2. Install: Double-click the downloaded file and click 'Install Font' in Font Book.
instructions.linux: 1. Download: Click the .ttf file under 'Assets' on the GitHub page. 2. Install: Copy files to ~/.local/share/fonts and run 'fc-cache -f'.

OtherDictionaries.description: A collection of useful online dictionaries and tools for Hán-Nôm research.

zi-tools.name: Zi.tools
zi-tools.description: A comprehensive tool for analyzing Chinese character components, etymology, and phonology.

nom-na-viet.name: Nôm Na Việt
nom-na-viet.description: Comprehensive Hán-Nôm dictionary with OCR, handwriting recognition, and a classical text reader.

thivien.name: Hán Việt Từ Điển Trích Dẫn (Thi Viện)
thivien.description: A popular online dictionary for Sino-Vietnamese characters, featuring detailed definitions and compound word lookups.

zdic.name: Zdic (Handian)
zdic.description: A comprehensive online Chinese character dictionary featuring detailed explanations, etymology, calligraphy, and phonology.

pleco.name: Pleco
pleco.description: A widely used Chinese dictionary app with handwriting recognition, OCR, and flashcards.

de-rhodes.name: De Rhodes Dictionary (OCR)
de-rhodes.description: OCR version of the Dictionarium Annamiticum Lusitanum et Latinum on Wikisource.

moe-variants.name: Dictionary of Chinese Character Variants (異體字字典)
moe-variants.description: An authoritative reference from Taiwan's Ministry of Education cataloguing variant forms of Chinese characters, with detailed annotations and historical sources.
